UK

Fully Funded Scholarships for Refugee Students in the UK

Published 21 Jun 20266 min read✍ MigrantScholar Team✓ Verified 21 Jun 2026
Advertisement

Introduction to Fully Funded Scholarships for Refugee Students in the UK

If you are a refugee student seeking to pursue higher education in the UK, there are several fully funded scholarships available to support your academic journey. These scholarships are designed to provide financial assistance to refugee students who are unable to afford the costs of studying in the UK. By applying for these scholarships, refugee students can focus on their studies and achieve their academic goals without worrying about financial constraints.

Who Qualifies for Refugee Scholarships in the UK

To qualify for refugee scholarships in the UK, you must hold a specific visa category, including:

* Humanitarian Protection visa

* Refugee visa

* Family Reunion visa

* Discretionary Leave to Remain visa

* Leave to Remain under the Syrian Vulnerable Persons Resettlement Scheme

* Leave to Remain under the Vulnerable Children's Resettlement Scheme

* Other visa categories that provide protection in the UK

Available Scholarships for Refugee Students

The Weidenfeld-Hoffmann Scholarships

The Weidenfeld-Hoffmann Scholarships provide full funding for graduate studies at the University of Oxford, covering university fees, living costs, and returns airfare. To be eligible, applicants must be applying for a graduate course at the University of Oxford and must have a strong academic and professional background. The deadline for applications is usually in January, and applicants can find more information on the University of Oxford website.

The Said Foundation Scholarships

The Said Foundation Scholarships provide full funding for postgraduate studies at participating UK universities, covering university fees, living costs, and returns airfare. To be eligible, applicants must be applying for a postgraduate course at a participating UK university and must have a strong academic and professional background. The deadline for applications is usually in November, and applicants can find more information on the Said Foundation website.

The Commonwealth Shared Scholarship Scheme

The Commonwealth Shared Scholarship Scheme provides full funding for master's studies at participating UK universities, covering university fees, living costs, and returns airfare. To be eligible, applicants must be applying for a master's course at a participating UK university and must have a strong academic and professional background. The deadline for applications is usually in December, and applicants can find more information on the Commonwealth Scholarship Commission website.

The Chevening Scholarships

The Chevening Scholarships provide full funding for postgraduate studies at any UK university, covering university fees, living costs, and returns airfare. To be eligible, applicants must be applying for a postgraduate course at any UK university and must have a strong academic and professional background. The deadline for applications is usually in November, and applicants can find more information on the Chevening website.

The Refugee Education UK Scholarships

The Refugee Education UK Scholarships provide full funding for undergraduate and postgraduate studies at participating UK universities, covering university fees, living costs, and returns airfare. To be eligible, applicants must be applying for an undergraduate or postgraduate course at a participating UK university and must have a strong academic and professional background. The deadline for applications is usually in June, and applicants can find more information on the Refugee Education UK website.

Application Steps for Refugee Scholarships

  • Research available scholarships and universities that you are eligible for.

  • Review the eligibility criteria and requirements for each scholarship.

  • Prepare your application documents, including transcripts, certificates, and language proficiency test results.

  • Submit your university application and scholarship application.

  • Wait for the application to be processed and a decision to be made.

  • Provide additional documents or information if requested by the scholarship provider.

  • Receive and accept the scholarship offer.

  • Prepare to travel to the UK and start your studies.

Documents Checklist for Refugee Scholarships

* Passport

* Visa documents

* University transcripts and certificates

* Language proficiency test results

* Personal statement and research proposal

* Academic references

* Proof of financial support

* Proof of refugee status

Frequently Asked Questions about Refugee Scholarships

  • What is the difference between a refugee and an asylum seeker?

A refugee is someone who has been recognized as a refugee by the UK government, while an asylum seeker is someone who is applying for refugee status. Both may be eligible for refugee scholarships, but the eligibility criteria may vary.

  • Can I apply for a refugee scholarship if I am already in the UK?

Yes, you can apply for a refugee scholarship if you are already in the UK, but you must meet the eligibility criteria and have a valid visa or immigration status.

  • How do I apply for a refugee scholarship?

You can apply for a refugee scholarship by submitting an online application to the scholarship provider or university, including all required documents and information.

  • What are the eligibility criteria for refugee scholarships?

The eligibility criteria for refugee scholarships vary, but typically include having a strong academic and professional background, meeting the language proficiency requirements, and having a valid visa or immigration status.

  • Can I apply for multiple refugee scholarships?

Yes, you can apply for multiple refugee scholarships, but you must meet the eligibility criteria for each scholarship and submit separate applications.

  • How long does it take to process a refugee scholarship application?

The processing time for a refugee scholarship application varies, but it can take several months or even years to receive a decision.

  • What are the benefits of a refugee scholarship?

The benefits of a refugee scholarship include full funding for university fees, living costs, and returns airfare, as well as opportunities for academic and professional development.

  • Where can I find more information about refugee scholarships?

You can find more information about refugee scholarships on the websites of scholarship providers, universities, and government organizations, such as the UK Government website and the University of Oxford website.

For more information and guidance on applying for scholarships as a refugee student, you can also visit the Refugee Council website. To access more resources and articles on scholarships for migrants, visit the Migrant Scholar blog.
